cases.pristine: in its original, spotless condition, immaculate and clean
cases.enunciate: to pronounce words clearly and distinctly
cases.ad-lib: to speak or perform without preparation, improvising
cases.recuperate: to recover from illness or exertion
cases.formidable: inspiring fear or respect through being powerful or capable
cases.incendiary: designed to cause fires, or tending to arouse strong feelings
cases.avarice: extreme greed for wealth or material gain
cases.hubris: excessive pride or self-confidence, especially leading to downfall
cases.chivalry: the medieval knightly code, or courteous behavior especially toward women
cases.delusional: believing things that are not true, holding false beliefs
cases.perpetrate: to carry out or commit a harmful or illegal act
cases.vanguard: the leading group in a movement or trend
cases.chivalrous: courteous and honorable, especially toward the weak or wronged
cases.countenance: a person's face or facial expression
cases.fleeting: lasting only a very short time
cases.steeped: thoroughly permeated or filled with a particular quality
cases.despot: a ruler with absolute power, especially a cruel one
cases.freewheeling: acting without concern for rules or consequences, unrestrained
cases.egotistical: excessively self-centered, having an exaggerated sense of self-importance
cases.abdicate: to formally give up a throne or position of power
cases.fidelity: faithfulness or loyalty to a person, cause, or belief
cases.mishmash: a confused mixture of different things
cases.soliloquy: a speech in which a character speaks their thoughts aloud, alone
cases.topsy-turvy: in a state of complete disorder or confusion
cases.dogmatic: asserting opinions as if they were unquestionably true
cases.levity: a lighthearted, humorous approach to something serious
cases.noncommittal: not expressing a clear opinion, avoiding commitment
cases.complacent: showing smug self-satisfaction with one's own achievements
cases.surmise: to guess or suppose something without complete evidence
cases.desultory: lacking a plan or purpose, half-hearted and disconnected
cases.androgynous: having both masculine and feminine characteristics, of indeterminate gender appearance
cases.trudge: to walk slowly and with heavy steps, especially with effort
cases.bewitch: to enchant or captivate someone completely
cases.demeanor: outward behavior or manner, especially toward others
cases.nimble: quick and light in movement or action
cases.high-strung: very nervous and easily upset, tense
cases.zealous: having great energy or enthusiasm in pursuit of a cause
cases.mediocrity: the quality of being only average, not very good
cases.premonition: a strong feeling that something is about to happen
cases.dichotomy: a division between two contradictory or opposed things
cases.prowess: great skill or expertise in a particular activity
cases.rouse: to stir someone into activity or action
cases.on tenterhooks: in a state of suspense or anxious anticipation
cases.impasse: a situation in which no progress can be made, a deadlock
cases.underhanded: acting in a secret or dishonest manner
cases.credence: acceptance that something is true or valid, belief
cases.somber: dark, gloomy, or serious in mood
cases.bandy about: to discuss or mention something casually or repeatedly
cases.hound: to pursue or harass someone relentlessly
cases.penitent: feeling or showing sorrow and regret for one's wrongdoing
cases.frailty: the condition of being weak or fragile
cases.oblivion: the state of being completely forgotten or destroyed
cases.meticulous: showing great attention to detail, very careful and precise
cases.chide: to scold or rebuke someone mildly
